title: APIC Controller Resource Exhaustion Watch
exclusions: Standalone Nexus platforms without APIC REST telemetry; leaf or spine CPU from SNMP alone without controller context (other use cases); deep guest-VM memory inside unrelated hypervisors without mapping to apic_id (separate compute use cases).
dataSources: `index=cisco_aci` (rename per governance) blending five sourcetypes: **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:apic_health`** (cluster heartbeats and controller posture), **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:proc_stats`** (**procEntity** / **procSysLoad** samples exposing **`cpuPct`**, **`memFree`**, and load facts), **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault`** (**faultInst** rows for resource codes), **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`** (OOM, flash, filesystem mnemonics), and **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:audit`** (**aaaModLR** maintenance or firmware changes). Primary class queries: **`GET https://<apic>/api/class/procEntity.json`** and **`GET https://<apic>/api/class/eqptStorage.json`**. Key Splunk fields: **`apic_id`**, **`cpuPct`**, **`memFree`**, **`diskUsed`**, **`inodePct`**.
app: **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) for **`cisco:aci:apic_health`**, **`cisco:aci:proc_stats`**, **`cisco:aci:fault`**, **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, and **`cisco:aci:audit`**; **Splunk REST API Modular Input** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) for **`/api/class/procEntity.json`** and **`/api/class/eqptStorage.json`** polling; **Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)); optional **Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) and **Splunk_TA_vmware** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) when VMware calendars explain controller memory noise.
spl:
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:proc_stats" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:apic_health") earliest=-2h@m latest=now
| eval apic_id=trim(coalesce(apic_id,apicId,apicName,fabricNode,node_id,nodeId,serial,""))
| eval cpuPct=tonumber(coalesce(cpuPct,cpu_pct,fiveMinAvg,procSysLoad5min,loadAvg5m,procLoad,""))
| eval memFree=tonumber(coalesce(memFree,mem_free_mb,memAvailMB,availMem,memAvailable,freeMemMB,""))
| eval diskUsed=tonumber(coalesce(diskUsed,disk_used_pct,usedPercent,storageUsed,eqptUsedPct,flashUsedPct,availDisk,""))
| eval inodePct=tonumber(coalesce(inodePct,inode_used_pct,inodeUtilPct,inodeUsed,pctInodeUsed,""))
| where len(apic_id)>0 AND (isnotnull(cpuPct) OR isnotnull(memFree) OR isnotnull(diskUsed) OR isnotnull(inodePct))
| bin _time span=5m
| stats latest(cpuPct) as cpu_pct latest(memFree) as mem_free_mb latest(diskUsed) as disk_used_pct latest(inodePct) as inode_pct values(sourcetype) as src_lane max(_time) as last_tick by _time apic_id
| eventstats latest(cpu_pct) as roll_cpu latest(mem_free_mb) as roll_mem latest(disk_used_pct) as roll_disk latest(inode_pct) as roll_inode by apic_id
| where roll_cpu>=88 OR roll_mem<=768 OR roll_disk>=85 OR roll_inode>=85 OR (isnull(roll_mem) AND roll_disk>=78)
| eval join_key="aci_apic_res_exh_23"
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" earliest=-2h@m latest=now
    | eval lc=lower(trim(coalesce(lc,lifecycleState,lifecycle,"")))
    | eval sev=lower(trim(coalesce(severity,fault_severity,code,"")))
    | eval open=if(match(lc,"cleared|resolved") OR match(sev,"cleared"),0,1)
    | where open=1 AND (match(_raw,"procEntity|procSysLoad|eqptStorage|eqptFlash|memory|disk|inode|resource|flash") OR match(lower(coalesce(code,cause,"")),"memory|disk|flash|inode"))
    | stats count as open_res_faults dc(code) as fault_codes dc(sev) as sev_spread
    | eval join_key="aci_apic_res_exh_23"
  ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" earliest=-2h@m latest=now
    | eval line=lower(_raw)
    | where match(line,"memory|oom|disk|inode|flash|apic|proc|storage|exhaust|space")
    | stats count as syslog_res_hits
    | eval join_key="aci_apic_res_exh_23"
  ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit" earliest=-2h@m latest=now
    | eval obj=lower(trim(coalesce(changingObj,ind,aaamodlr_obj,"")))
    | where match(obj,"fwrepo|polic|maint|image|upgrade|eqpt|proc|sys/firmware|obs")
    | stats count as audit_chg_hits dc(obj) as touched_mos
    | eval join_key="aci_apic_res_exh_23"
  ]
| eval risk=case(roll_inode>=92 OR roll_disk>=92,"critical_storage", roll_cpu>=95 OR roll_mem<=384,"critical_compute", roll_disk>=88 OR roll_inode>=88,"storage_pressure",1==1,"capacity_warning")
| table last_tick apic_id cpu_pct mem_free_mb disk_used_pct inode_pct risk open_res_faults fault_codes sev_spread syslog_res_hits audit_chg_hits touched_mos src_lane roll_cpu roll_mem roll_disk roll_inode
| sort - roll_disk - roll_cpu
description: Detects Cisco APIC controller resource strain by normalizing **cpuPct**, **memFree**, **diskUsed**, and **inodePct** from **cisco:aci:proc_stats** and **cisco:aci:apic_health**, then joining **cisco:aci:fault**, **cisco:aci:syslog**, and **cisco:aci:audit** so operators can separate **procEntity** / **eqptStorage** saturation from benign upgrade or import noise before policy push brownouts or fault processing stalls appear.
value: Protects automation and change-window outcomes by surfacing controller saturation early enough to grow flash budgets, schedule log cleanup, or pause bulk imports before the cluster loses headroom to commit contracts and clear faults—avoiding silent degradation that still pings while intent delivery stops.
implementation: Deploy Splunkbase 4022 inputs plus 1546 HTTPS class pulls for **procEntity** and **eqptStorage**, normalize FIELDALIAS for **apic_id**, **cpuPct**, **memFree**, **diskUsed**, and **inodePct**, schedule **aci_uc18123_apic_resource_exhaustion_core** every 5–15m, and adopt 7777 macros after index rename; add 4856/1810 only when VMware calendars explain correlated memory swings.
visualization: Single-value gauges for cluster max disk and inode with sparkline **cpuPct** per **apic_id**; overlay timechart **memFree** versus **diskUsed**; sortable table of **apic_id**, **risk**, join-lane counts, and fault codes.
schema: **Performance** carries **cpuPct**, **memFree**, **diskUsed**, **inodePct**, and time-bucket KPIs per **apic_id**; **Inventory** anchors **apic_id** to serial or node metadata when mapped; **Alerts** aligns open faults with **faultInst**-style severity context; **Change** ties **aaaModLR** objects to authorized maintenance or imports.

references.title: Cisco APIC REST API Class Queries (KB)
Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk (Splunkbase 7777)

detailedImplementation:
**Prerequisites**

Production APIC clusters must expose HTTPS on tcp/443 with read-only RBAC sufficient to query **procEntity**, **procSysLoad**, **eqptStorage**, and **eqptFlash** MOs without granting configuration privileges that would let accidental writes land during investigations. Install **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) and confirm five sourcetypes land in the governed index: **cisco:aci:apic_health** (cluster health snapshots and periodic controller posture), **cisco:aci:proc_stats** (class-query samples that carry **cpuPct**, **memFree**, and related processor load facts), **cisco:aci:fault** (faultInst narratives referencing resource-type codes), **cisco:aci:syslog** (APIC mnemonics for memory, disk, inode, or flash pressure), and **cisco:aci:audit** (aaaModLR rows for maintenance, firmware, repository, and policy operations). Normalize FIELDALIAS so Splunk consistently receives **apic_id**, **cpuPct**, **memFree**, **diskUsed**, and **inodePct** despite JSON drift across APIC releases.

Deploy **Splunk REST API Modular Input** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) for GET `https://<apic>/api/class/procEntity.json` and GET `https://<apic>/api/class/eqptStorage.json` on 60–180s cadence when TA guidance says supplemental class pulls are needed to capture flash versus rotating-media totals that **cisco:aci:proc_stats** does not always duplicate. Map children such as **eqptFlash** through Visore before widening rsp-subtree pulls, and throttle politely to avoid 429 storms during concurrent GUI sessions. Align **Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) macros with the same index label this UC references. Optional **Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) and **Splunk_TA_vmware** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) timestamps help separate VM migration noise from genuine controller memory oscillation when APIC guests share hypervisor hosts.

Retain hot storage for at least fourteen days; combined REST polls, faults, syslog, and audit streams can emit hundreds of thousands of events weekly in large fabrics. Annotate runbooks with each APIC model’s RAM size so **memFree** thresholds cite MB budgets rather than one-size guesses.

**Telemetry hygiene** — Resource searches go blind if **cisco:aci:proc_stats** arrives minutes late while **cisco:aci:apic_health** stays real-time: operators chase phantom relief. Document every modular-input hostname beside CMDB rows so DNS drift or VIP stickiness during failovers does not masquerade as metric loss. Deduplicate REST payloads that replay identical modTs, verify syslog time zones, and watch modular-input logs for HTTP 429 responses that pause **procEntity** samples while **cisco:aci:syslog** still flows.

**Fabric context** — APIC nodes host the policy repository, fault datastore, GUI stack, and upgrade orchestration. Brownouts often begin as slower dashboards, sluggish fault clears, or hung policy commits while ICMP to leaves still succeeds. Splunk focuses on normalized **cpuPct**, **memFree**, **diskUsed**, and **inodePct** per apic_id so NetOps can prioritize controller care before automation stops answering.

**Step 1** — Configure data collection

Create read-only APIC accounts with NETWORK-style roles that allow class queries for **procEntity**, **procSysLoad**, **eqptStorage**, and **eqptFlash** without write entitlement. Prefer rsp-subtree scoped payloads to limit HTTP body size; widen only when Visore proves missing fields. Run **cisco:aci:proc_stats** inputs between 60–300s according to vendor guidance—sub-minute polling spotlights flash wear but increases API load. Enable remote syslog from each APIC with filters that preserve memory, disk, inode, and flash mnemonics without mirroring every debug line. Stream aaaModLR into **cisco:aci:audit** preserving ind DNs that touch maintenance MOs, firmware bundles, repositories, or observability policies.

After upgrades, spot-check three fields in Search against the APIC System dashboards: **cpuPct** versus five-minute averages, **memFree** versus available RAM panels, and **diskUsed**/**inodePct** versus **eqptStorage** and **eqptFlash** widgets. Drift here signals props skew rather than hardware failure.

**Step 2** — Create the search

Paste the SPL from the spl field, save as **aci_uc18123_apic_resource_exhaustion_core**, wrap indexes in macros if governance requires it, then tune roll_cpu, roll_mem, roll_disk, and roll_inode using two weeks of baseline per site.

```spl
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:proc_stats" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:apic_health") earliest=-2h@m latest=now
| eval apic_id=trim(coalesce(apic_id,apicId,apicName,fabricNode,node_id,nodeId,serial,""))
| eval cpuPct=tonumber(coalesce(cpuPct,cpu_pct,fiveMinAvg,procSysLoad5min,loadAvg5m,procLoad,""))
| eval memFree=tonumber(coalesce(memFree,mem_free_mb,memAvailMB,availMem,memAvailable,freeMemMB,""))
| eval diskUsed=tonumber(coalesce(diskUsed,disk_used_pct,usedPercent,storageUsed,eqptUsedPct,flashUsedPct,availDisk,""))
| eval inodePct=tonumber(coalesce(inodePct,inode_used_pct,inodeUtilPct,inodeUsed,pctInodeUsed,""))
| where len(apic_id)>0 AND (isnotnull(cpuPct) OR isnotnull(memFree) OR isnotnull(diskUsed) OR isnotnull(inodePct))
| bin _time span=5m
| stats latest(cpuPct) as cpu_pct latest(memFree) as mem_free_mb latest(diskUsed) as disk_used_pct latest(inodePct) as inode_pct values(sourcetype) as src_lane max(_time) as last_tick by _time apic_id
| eventstats latest(cpu_pct) as roll_cpu latest(mem_free_mb) as roll_mem latest(disk_used_pct) as roll_disk latest(inode_pct) as roll_inode by apic_id
| where roll_cpu>=88 OR roll_mem<=768 OR roll_disk>=85 OR roll_inode>=85 OR (isnull(roll_mem) AND roll_disk>=78)
| eval join_key="aci_apic_res_exh_23"
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" earliest=-2h@m latest=now
    | eval lc=lower(trim(coalesce(lc,lifecycleState,lifecycle,"")))
    | eval sev=lower(trim(coalesce(severity,fault_severity,code,"")))
    | eval open=if(match(lc,"cleared|resolved") OR match(sev,"cleared"),0,1)
    | where open=1 AND (match(_raw,"procEntity|procSysLoad|eqptStorage|eqptFlash|memory|disk|inode|resource|flash") OR match(lower(coalesce(code,cause,"")),"memory|disk|flash|inode"))
    | stats count as open_res_faults dc(code) as fault_codes dc(sev) as sev_spread
    | eval join_key="aci_apic_res_exh_23"
  ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" earliest=-2h@m latest=now
    | eval line=lower(_raw)
    | where match(line,"memory|oom|disk|inode|flash|apic|proc|storage|exhaust|space")
    | stats count as syslog_res_hits
    | eval join_key="aci_apic_res_exh_23"
  ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit" earliest=-2h@m latest=now
    | eval obj=lower(trim(coalesce(changingObj,ind,aaamodlr_obj,"")))
    | where match(obj,"fwrepo|polic|maint|image|upgrade|eqpt|proc|sys/firmware|obs")
    | stats count as audit_chg_hits dc(obj) as touched_mos
    | eval join_key="aci_apic_res_exh_23"
  ]
| eval risk=case(roll_inode>=92 OR roll_disk>=92,"critical_storage", roll_cpu>=95 OR roll_mem<=384,"critical_compute", roll_disk>=88 OR roll_inode>=88,"storage_pressure",1==1,"capacity_warning")
| table last_tick apic_id cpu_pct mem_free_mb disk_used_pct inode_pct risk open_res_faults fault_codes sev_spread syslog_res_hits audit_chg_hits touched_mos src_lane roll_cpu roll_mem roll_disk roll_inode
| sort - roll_disk - roll_cpu
```

**Understanding this SPL** — The union of **cisco:aci:proc_stats** and **cisco:aci:apic_health** coalesces vendor keys into **apic_id**, **cpuPct**, **memFree**, **diskUsed**, and **inodePct**. Five-minute bins absorb REST jitter while preserving trend visibility. eventstats projects rolling latest values per **apic_id** so thresholds evaluate sustained stress, not a single blip. **Join** lanes summarize open resource-oriented faults, syslog keyword hits, and audit object touches for the same interval; empty joins flag telemetry gaps rather than automatic all-clear. risk bucketing routes pages toward storage versus compute saturation teams.

**Step 3** — Validate

Compare each alerted **apic_id** against the APIC GUI monitoring views for CPU, memory, disk, and inode parity within one poll interval. Run `| metadata type=sourcetype index=cisco_aci` (rename per policy) to confirm recentTime across all five sourcetypes. When open_res_faults > 0, open Faults UI entries referencing **procEntity**, **eqptStorage**, **eqptFlash**, or memory-related codes during the same window. If audit_chg_hits spikes, capture operator identities from aaaModLR before assuming hardware faults.

**Step 4** — Operationalize

Publish per-**apic_id** drilldowns linking to saved searches scoped by fabric pod and serial. Schedule the core search every five to fifteen minutes with throttling per **apic_id** to suppress duplicate paging during rolling upgrades. Export weekly CSV snapshots of **diskUsed** and **inodePct** for capacity reviews. Mirror alert subjects into ITSM with links to fault codes and audit objects so reviewers see authorized changes adjacent to the metric slip.

**Step 5** — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

**Visualization** — Lead with single-value gauges for max **roll_disk** and max **roll_inode** across the cluster plus sparklines of **cpuPct** per **apic_id**. Second panel: timechart overlay **memFree** and **diskUsed** split by **apic_id**. Third panel: sortable table listing **apic_id**, **last_tick**, **cpu_pct**, **mem_free_mb**, **disk_used_pct**, **inode_pct**, **risk**, **open_res_faults**, **fault_codes**, **syslog_res_hits**, **audit_chg_hits**, **touched_mos**, and **src_lane**; highlight rows where join counts are zero yet **roll_disk** or **roll_inode** violates policy.

**Alert** — Fire when **roll_disk** ≥85% or **roll_inode** ≥85% for two consecutive evaluation windows, or **roll_cpu** ≥88% with open_res_faults > 0, or **roll_mem** ≤768 MB (tune per model) with matching **syslog_res_hits**. Throttle forty-five minutes per **apic_id**. Payloads list all four headline metrics, **risk** tier, fault-code spread, top audit objects, and URLs to saved fault and audit investigations. Recovery when **roll_disk** <78% and **roll_inode** <75% for four windows with quiet resource fault lanes.

**Failure modes** — Expired aaaRefresh tokens cause 401/403 responses—repair REST authentication before retuning thresholds. HTTP 429 storms require backoff or spreading polls across follower APICs. Null **memFree** after upgrade usually means renamed JSON keys—refresh FIELDALIAS from sampled events. Inode alerts with flat **diskUsed** imply verbose logging or core dumps; check syslog facilities before RMA. Healthy joins with zero fault matches may still reflect real pressure—do not auto-close on empty fault subsearches alone. Splunkbase 7777 starter content is trustworthy only when macros target the same index as this SPL.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the three policy computers that steer your fabric and warn you when they run short on memory, disk, or file-table space, so control work does not stall without a clear reason.
hardware: Cisco APIC M3/L4/L5 cluster appliances (three-node production minimum)
knownFalsePositives:
**apic_rolling_upgrade** — Coordinated image work and repository expansion temporarily raise CPU and flash writes so disk percentages climb without long-run exhaustion. Require two consecutive breaches outside maintenance windows or bind suppression to change tickets that cite firmware tasks before paging hardware.

**follower_poll_skew** — REST inputs pinned to one APIC FQDN can briefly show followers idle while the leader saturates during VIP failovers. Compare each apic_id side-by-side in the same buckets before replacing nodes.

**compact_log_burst_logrotate** — Scheduled compaction or log rotation lifts CPU and flash I/O for minutes only. Ignore single-bucket violations when audit rows mention observability or diag objects and syslog shows housekeeping mnemonics.

**verbose_audit_inode_drain** — Verbose AAA or mirrored syslog can consume inodes faster than disk percentages move. Tune remote logging levels and retention on lab fabrics before declaring controller fault on inode alone.

**rest_429_metric_hole** — Brief API throttling drops procEntity samples while heartbeats continue, mimicking sudden CPU relief. Validate modular-input logs for 429 before trusting missing cpuPct as recovery.

**policy_import_memory_spike** — Large policy imports inflate memory and flash usage until compilation settles; faults may stay quiet. Cross-reference audit_chg_hits for policy DNs and delay pages until documented import windows close.

**double_modular_input_dupes** — Two modular inputs hitting the same class URL double events and skew latest() math. Deduplicate on apic_id, hashed modTs, and sourcetype before alerting.

**lab_hypervisor_overcommit** — Virtualized APIC labs co-resident with noisy neighbors show memory jitter uncorrelated with fabric load. Lower severity when hypervisor dashboards prove host overcommit rather than policy DB growth.

**issu_counter_quiet** — Image upgrades or ISSU can suppress fault generation for a poll while counters reset, so joins look empty even though flash writers are busy. Ignore one interval bounded by maintenance records before treating the pattern as telemetry failure.
